name: ui-ux-pro-max
description: UI/UX design intelligence. 50 styles, 21 palettes, 50 font pairings, 20 charts, 9 stacks (React, Next.js, Vue, Svelte, SwiftUI, React Native, Flutter, Tailwind, shadcn/ui). Actions: plan, build, create, design, implement, review, fix, improve, optimize, enhance, refactor, check UI/UX code. Projects: website, landing page, dashboard, admin panel, e-commerce, SaaS, portfolio, blog, mobile app, .html, .tsx, .vue, .svelte. Elements: button, modal, navbar, sidebar, card, table, form, chart. Styles: glassmorphism, claymorphism, minimalism, brutalism, neumorphism, bento grid, dark mode, responsive, skeuomorphism, flat design. Topics: color palette, accessibility, animation, layout, typography, font pairing, spacing, hover, shadow, gradient. Integrations: shadcn/ui MCP for component search and examples.
capabilities: Search and retrieve 50+ UI design styles including glassmorphism, minimalism, brutalism, and neumorphism
Recommend accessible color palettes with proper contrast ratios for 21 product categories
Suggest font pairings from Google Fonts with CSS and Tailwind configuration
Provide stack-specific implementation guidelines for React, Vue, Svelte, Next.js, and mobile frameworks
Generate complete design systems with reasoning from priority-based rules
Check UI code against 99 UX guidelines covering accessibility, touch targets, and performance
author: ZhanlinCui
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 1395 patterns across 28 files, but all findings are false positives. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are triggered by BM25 scoring math (log functions) in a text search engine, not actual cryptography. 'Hardcoded URL' findings are documentation URLs in CSV data files. 'External commands' are bash examples in documentation showing how to run the Python search tool. The skill performs local file operations on its own data files with no network calls, no dynamic code execution, and no user input processing.
valueStatement: Creating professional UI/UX designs requires deep knowledge of accessibility, color theory, typography, and stack-specific patterns. This skill provides a searchable database of 50+ design styles, color palettes, font pairings, and implementation guidelines to help you build polished interfaces faster.
userTitle: Generate Professional UI/UX Designs with Design System Intelligence
limitations: Requires Python 3.x installed to run the search CLI tool
Design recommendations are based on static CSV data, not live design trend analysis
Does not generate actual UI code, only provides guidelines and recommendations
Stack guidelines cover common patterns but may not address edge cases or latest framework versions

useCases.targetUser: Frontend Developer Building a New Dashboard
useCases.title: Frontend Developer Building a New Dashboard
useCases.description: A developer needs to create an admin dashboard for a SaaS product. They use the skill to get design system recommendations for 'SaaS dashboard', retrieve accessible color palettes for data-heavy interfaces, and get React-specific guidelines for performance optimization.

useCases.targetUser: Solo Founder Creating a Landing Page
useCases.title: Solo Founder Creating a Landing Page
useCases.description: A non-designer founder needs a professional landing page for their startup. They search for 'beauty spa wellness service' to get a complete design system with style, colors, typography, and landing page patterns, then use Tailwind CSS guidelines to implement it.

useCases.title: Designer Ensuring Accessibility Compliance
useCases.description: A designer wants to ensure their UI meets WCAG guidelines. They query the ux domain for 'accessibility contrast focus' to get specific rules on color contrast ratios, focus states, and keyboard navigation with code examples.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Style Search
promptTemplates.prompt: Search for design styles matching 'minimalist dark mode dashboard'. Show me the top 3 style recommendations with their color schemes and key characteristics.

promptTemplates.title: Complete Design System Generation
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a complete design system for a 'fintech crypto trading dashboard'. Include product pattern, UI style, color palette, typography, and effects. Name the project 'CryptoVault'.

promptTemplates.title: Accessibility Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this button component for accessibility issues: [paste code]. Check against WCAG guidelines for color contrast, focus states, and touch target size. Provide specific fixes.

promptTemplates.title: Stack-Specific Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: I'm building a responsive navbar with Tailwind CSS. Search for 'navbar responsive floating' guidelines in the html-tailwind stack and provide implementation code with proper aria attributes.

bestPractices: Always start with --design-system flag to get comprehensive recommendations with reasoning before implementing
Check accessibility guidelines first - color contrast (4.5:1), focus states, and touch targets (44x44px minimum)
Use stack-specific searches to get implementation patterns that follow framework conventions
antiPatterns: Using emoji as UI icons instead of proper SVG icon libraries like Heroicons or Lucide
Implementing hover states with scale transforms that cause layout shift
Using transparent glass cards (bg-white/10) in light mode where they become invisible

faq.question: Do I need to install any dependencies to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need Python 3.x installed. The skill uses a Python CLI tool to search its CSV database. No additional Python packages are required as it uses only standard library modules.

faq.question: Can this skill generate actual UI code?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides design guidelines, recommendations, and patterns. It does not generate production code. Use the recommendations to inform your implementation in your preferred framework.

faq.question: What technology stacks are supported?
faq.answer: The skill covers 9 stacks: html-tailwind (default), React, Next.js, Vue, Svelte, SwiftUI, React Native, Flutter, shadcn/ui, and Jetpack Compose. Each has specific guidelines for patterns, performance, and best practices.

faq.question: How do I save a design system for later use?
faq.answer: Use the --persist flag with --design-system to create a MASTER.md file with your design system. You can also create page-specific overrides using the --page flag for hierarchical retrieval across sessions.

faq.question: Can I search for specific design elements like charts or icons?
faq.answer: Yes, use the --domain flag with values like 'chart' for chart types, 'icons' for icon recommendations, 'typography' for font pairings, 'color' for palettes, or 'ux' for usability guidelines.

faq.question: Is the design data updated regularly?
faq.answer: The skill uses static CSV files bundled with the repository. For the latest design trends, you may need to update the skill repository or supplement with additional research.
